name: genomics_governance_procurement_millhaven_auto_ben
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: genomics governance procurement millhaven auto ben. The moral tension is explicitly framed as autonomy versus beneficence: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. A whistleblower tip has reached a reporter; silence reads as cover-up, while a rushed statement reads as recklessness. Legal and comms are on hold; you must record a decision in the incident audit trail within the next fifteen minutes. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs.

agents.name: Darius Diallo
agents.identity: I am Darius Diallo, the accountable decision-maker on this Genomics Governance bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes beneficence whenever someone invokes autonomy.

agents.name: Amina Kim
agents.identity: I am Amina Kim, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so beneficence is not sacrificed by default just because autonomy feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect beneficence.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The clock on this bridge is running out. With partial evidence, Darius Diallo must decide under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Darius Diallo do?
